other_observers: Van Remsen who agreed with ID
description: Moderately bright green back, nape and crown extending down into auriculars forming a moderately sharp border with bright white throat. Eyering narrow, white and weak to absent in front. Wingbars tinged in dull yellow-green. Tertials black with sharply contrasting white borders. Primaries long. Tail white and proportionately shortish. Crown relatively flat (meaning modestly rounded, not peaked/crested). Belly washed in pale yellow, chest with olive wash on sides. When found, giving typical ALFL "Pleep" call, when recording played by Van Remsen, the bird started to sing typical wee-BEE-o
